main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Mono-DynGS: a method for dynamic scene reconstruction from monocular videos without known camera poses","3D Gaussians Splatting (3DGS) for scene representation","Efficient camera initialization module via relative camera pose estimation between image pairs using SE(3) transformations of 3D Gaussians","Unified representation for both static and dynamic regions using a Hexplane-based encoder (six planes: (x,y), (x,z), (y,z), (x,t), (y,t), (z,t))","Deformation field to model motion of deformable objects","Depth and optical flow regularization during optimization"],"problems":["Dynamic 3D scene reconstruction from monocular videos without known camera poses","Robust camera pose estimation in the presence of dynamic objects and motion blur","Efficient and high-quality novel-view synthesis for dynamic scenes","Unified modeling of static and dynamic regions in dynamic scene reconstruction"],"datasets":["DyCheck (Gao et al., 2022)","NVIDIA DynamicNeRF (Gao et al., 2021)","MPI Sintel (Butler et al., 2012)"],"metrics":["Dynamic novel-view synthesis quality (e.g., PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS)","Camera pose estimation accuracy"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to achieve high-quality dynamic 3D scene reconstruction from monocular videos without known camera poses using a unified 3D Gaussians representation.","Proposes a unified representation for static and dynamic regions, enabling information sharing and improved reconstruction quality.","Introduces an efficient camera initialization module based on relative pose estimation between image pairs using 3D Gaussians, improving optimization speed and robustness.","Employs a Hexplane-based encoder to disentangle and jointly optimize static background, camera motion, and dynamic object deformation.","Incorporates depth and optical flow regularization to enhance geometric consistency during optimization.","Demonstrates superior performance over prior methods (with and without known camera poses) on multiple dynamic scene datasets."]}

methods: Mono-DynGS: a method for dynamic scene reconstruction from monocular videos without known camera poses
3D Gaussians Splatting (3DGS) for scene representation
Efficient camera initialization module via relative camera pose estimation between image pairs using SE(3) transformations of 3D Gaussians
Unified representation for both static and dynamic regions using a Hexplane-based encoder (six planes: (x,y), (x,z), (y,z), (x,t), (y,t), (z,t))
Deformation field to model motion of deformable objects
Depth and optical flow regularization during optimization
problems: Dynamic 3D scene reconstruction from monocular videos without known camera poses
Robust camera pose estimation in the presence of dynamic objects and motion blur
Efficient and high-quality novel-view synthesis for dynamic scenes
Unified modeling of static and dynamic regions in dynamic scene reconstruction
metrics: Dynamic novel-view synthesis quality (e.g., PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS)
novelty_claims: First to achieve high-quality dynamic 3D scene reconstruction from monocular videos without known camera poses using a unified 3D Gaussians representation.
Proposes a unified representation for static and dynamic regions, enabling information sharing and improved reconstruction quality.
Introduces an efficient camera initialization module based on relative pose estimation between image pairs using 3D Gaussians, improving optimization speed and robustness.
Employs a Hexplane-based encoder to disentangle and jointly optimize static background, camera motion, and dynamic object deformation.
Incorporates depth and optical flow regularization to enhance geometric consistency during optimization.
Demonstrates superior performance over prior methods (with and without known camera poses) on multiple dynamic scene datasets.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["COLMAP-Free 3D Gaussian Splatting (CF-3DGS): sequentially processes video frames, progressively growing a 3D Gaussian set without pre-computed camera poses, leveraging explicit point cloud representation and temporal continuity","Two-step procedure for each frame: (1) construct a local 3D Gaussians set and learn an affine transformation (relative camera pose) between consecutive frames using neural rendering gradients; (2) aggregate current frame into a global 3D Gaussians set and optimize with all previous frames and poses"],"problems":["Novel view synthesis and 3D scene reconstruction without reliance on pre-computed camera poses (i.e., without SfM preprocessing like COLMAP)","Joint optimization of 3D structure and camera poses, especially under large camera motion changes","Reducing failure cases and time consumption associated with traditional SfM methods in challenging scenes (textureless, repetitive regions)"],"datasets":["Tanks and Temples dataset","CO3D dataset (randomly selected videos)"],"metrics":["View synthesis quality (likely PSNR/SSIM, though not explicitly stated)","Camera pose estimation accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"View synthesis quality (Tanks and Temples, CO3D)","value":"Significantly better than previous approaches without pre-computed camera poses"},{"metric":"Camera pose estimation accuracy (small motion)","value":"On par with Nope-NeRF"},{"metric":"Camera pose estimation accuracy (large motion, e.g., 360-degree videos in CO3D)","value":"Outperforms all previous approaches by a large margin"}],"novelty_claims":["First method to perform 3D Gaussian Splatting for novel view synthesis without any SfM (COLMAP) preprocessing","Sequential, frame-by-frame growing of 3D Gaussians using explicit point cloud representation and temporal continuity, enabling direct optimization of camera poses and scene structure","Significantly improved performance in both view synthesis and camera pose estimation, especially under large camera motion, compared to prior methods that do not use pre-computed camera poses"]}

methods: COLMAP-Free 3D Gaussian Splatting (CF-3DGS): sequentially processes video frames, progressively growing a 3D Gaussian set without pre-computed camera poses, leveraging explicit point cloud representation and temporal continuity

Two-step procedure for each frame: (1) construct a local 3D Gaussians set and learn an affine transformation (relative camera pose) between consecutive frames using neural rendering gradients; (2) aggregate current frame into a global 3D Gaussians set and optimize with all previous frames and poses
problems: Novel view synthesis and 3D scene reconstruction without reliance on pre-computed camera poses (i.e., without SfM preprocessing like COLMAP)
Joint optimization of 3D structure and camera poses, especially under large camera motion changes
Reducing failure cases and time consumption associated with traditional SfM methods in challenging scenes (textureless, repetitive regions)
metrics: View synthesis quality (likely PSNR/SSIM, though not explicitly stated)

results.metric: View synthesis quality (Tanks and Temples, CO3D)
results.value: Significantly better than previous approaches without pre-computed camera poses
On par with Nope-NeRF

results.metric: Camera pose estimation accuracy (large motion, e.g., 360-degree videos in CO3D)
results.value: Outperforms all previous approaches by a large margin

novelty_claims: First method to perform 3D Gaussian Splatting for novel view synthesis without any SfM (COLMAP) preprocessing
Sequential, frame-by-frame growing of 3D Gaussians using explicit point cloud representation and temporal continuity, enabling direct optimization of camera poses and scene structure
Significantly improved performance in both view synthesis and camera pose estimation, especially under large camera motion, compared to prior methods that do not use pre-computed camera poses
